gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/255)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/255)
text: رَجُلٍ شَهِدَ عَلَى شَهَادَةِ رَجُلٍ فَجَاءَ الرَّجُلُ فَقَالَ لَمْ أُشْهِدْهُ فَقَالَ تَجُوزُ شَهَادَةُ أَعْدَلِهِمَا وَ لَوْ كَانَ أَعْدَلُهُمَا وَاحِداً لَمْ تَجُزْ شَهَادَتُهُ عَدَالَةً فِيهِمَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Yunus, from Ibn Sinan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who testified upon a testimony of another man. So the man comes over, so he said to him, ‘I did not (appoint him) as a witness (on my behalf)’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The one who is more just from the two would be allowed (to give testimony), but if (there is only) one just (to be found), his testimony would not be allowed in fairness to both of them’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Yunus from b Sinan who has narrated the following: “This is about the case of a man who bears testimony to the testimony of another witness but then the man says that he had not appointed him as witness. Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘The testimony of the one who is more just is admissible but if such man of justice is one man then his testimony is not acceptable because of fairness for both.’”
